Yes, daily walks are highly effective for managing blood pressure. Regular physical activity, such as a 30-minute brisk walk most days of the week, helps your heart pump blood more efficiently and can lower your resting blood pressure by 4 to 9 mmHg. It also helps reduce stress, which is another factor that can contribute to high blood pressure.
